PgUp is a PostgreSQL migration tool using plain SQL for transaction-safe schema changes


init|initialize <PROJECTDIR> [options]

Creates a new PgUp project structure with necessary files for PostgreSQL deployment.

Arguments:  
  <PROJECTDIR>             The target directory where the PgUp project files and deployment scripts will be created.  
 
Options:  
  --template               Specifies the template used to initialize the project. Default is 'basic'.  
  --trace-level|--trace     Trace level  
 


deploy <PROJECTFILE> [options]

Deploys a PostgreSQL database using the specified project file and configurations.

Arguments:  
  <PROJECTFILE>            The PgUp project file defining the database structure and deployment settings.  
 
Options:  
  --host|-h                The hostname or IP address of the PostgreSQL server. Default is 'localhost'.  
  --port|-p                The hostname or IP address of the PostgreSQL server. Default is 'localhost'.  
  --username|--user|-u     The PostgreSQL user with permissions to create roles and databases.  
  --password|--pwd|-p      The password for the PostgreSQL user.  
  --maintenance-database|-mdb     The maintenance database to use during the deployment process. Default is 'postgres'.  
  --parameters|--parameter|-param     Key-value pairs to override default parameters in the project file.  
  --timeout                The maximum time to retry deployment in case of transient errors. Default is '1 minute'.  
  --overwrite              Drops the existing database and recreates it. All data will be lost.  
  --force                  Suppresses confirmation prompts when overwriting the database. Overrides safety checks.  
  --trace-level|--trace     Trace level
